summary: Backend testing of case-insensitive username duplicate registration bug fix. All 5 tests passed (100%). Case-insensitive dup registration is rejected (new & pre-existing users), login is case-insensitive returning the SAME user.id across case variants, duplicate email still rejected, and existing testbugfix1/password1 regression login works.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix correctly checks both new `username_lower` field AND legacy `username` via case-insensitive regex — good backward compatibility.
Recommend future work: enforce lowercasing at DB layer with schema validation to prevent regressions.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral users with uuid suffixes (bugcheckA*, BugChk*Xyz, emailtest*_*) — not cleaned up but isolated by uuid.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_case_insensitive_auth.py exercises captcha, register, login flows with helper functions solve_captcha/register/login. Use these helpers for any future auth tests.
rca of the issue: N/A — fix verified. Bug was fixed by adding username_lower field + case-insensitive regex fallback in register/login, plus unique sparse index on username_lower.
